mito.walk8_down.styleNotes:
hd_walk8_256: Original high-resolution pixel-art PLAYER WALK CYCLE sprite sheet for a bright mythical-Japan action RPG, top-down 3/4 view, facing DOWN (toward the viewer, face visible). the hero Mito: an androgynous 15-year-old wanderer with ink-black short hair tied into a tiny low ponytail at the nape, a vermilion braided-cord hair ornament, and calm amber eyes. Outfit (bright fantasy-Japanese traveler, NOT samurai, NOT ninja): white tube-sleeved top, a short indigo-blue haori jacket with small vermilion braided-cord knots on the sleeves and hem, loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties, light travel sandals, a vermilion braided cord worn as a belt-sash with a small tassel, and a necklace with three pale-green magatama beads. At his left hip hangs a sheathed short ritual sword in a WHITE scabbard wrapped with a thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers - it reads as a festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana. CRITICAL PROPORTION RULE: the proportions of the attached reference character EXACTLY: about 2.4 heads tall, sturdy grounded build with a solid chest, thick limbs and a broad stable stance - NOT slim, NOT lanky, NOT taller than the reference. Every frame keeps EXACTLY the same head size, the same total height and the same sturdy body width. SWORD RULE: the white katana scabbard stays sheathed at his LEFT hip and is drawn in the SAME steady position, angle and length relative to the body in EVERY frame - it must not drift left or right, must not rotate, must not lengthen or shorten, must not float away from the hip, and is NEVER held in the hand; only the body's natural walk motion moves. 8-frame WALK CYCLE with proper alternating footsteps: frame1 = CONTACT A (his LEFT foot planted forward, right foot back, arms counter-swing), frame2 = weight drop (body 2-3px lower), frame3 = PASSING (feet together under the body, body at mid height), frame4 = push-off (body 2-3px higher), frame5 = CONTACT B (his RIGHT foot planted forward, left foot back - the MIRROR step of frame1), frame6 = weight drop, frame7 = PASSING (feet together), frame8 = push-off. The forward foot MUST alternate: left foot leads in frame1, right foot leads in frame5. Arms swing opposite to the legs each step. Both feet clearly visible and readable against the ground. The haori hem and the vermilion cord tassels sway naturally with each step. STRICT CELL CONTAINMENT: no body part, no sword piece, no stray fragment may cross a cell border or float detached anywhere on the sheet; every drawn shape belongs to the single character inside its own cell. STRICT SHEET LAYOUT: a clean 4x2 grid (4 columns, 2 rows), 8 cells of EQUAL size, exactly ONE character per cell at the SAME size and SAME scale, centered with clear empty margin on all four sides, feet on the same ground line in every cell, head at the same height in every cell. TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right. No grid lines, no text.
Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI.
